description:
You are a security engineer in charge of doing an emergency credential-rotation.  
    The application keeps its secrets in this file:

        /home/user/app/config/settings.ini

    Present **initial** contents of that file (for your reference):

        [database]
        user = app_user
        password = oldDBpwd123

        [api]
        key = OLDAPIKEY0987654321

        [general]
        debug = false

    Required rotation targets
    --------------------------
    1. Replace the value of the password key in the [database] section with:  S3cureDBp@55w0rd!
    2. Replace the value of the key     key in the [api]      section with:  A1b2C3d4E5F6G7H8

    Directory & log file
    --------------------
    • If it does not already exist, create the directory  

          /home/user/rotation_logs

    • Inside that directory create a **plain-text log file** named exactly  

          rotation_20230918_120000.log

    Log-file format (the automated grader will check it byte-for-byte):
        Each credential changed must be recorded on its own line using this template  
            section:key:OLD_VALUE->NEW_VALUE  

        No extra spaces, no blank lines, newline-terminated (Unix LF) after each entry.  
        Therefore the final file must contain **exactly** these two lines:

            database:password:oldDBpwd123->S3cureDBp@55w0rd!
            api:key:OLDAPIKEY0987654321->A1b2C3d4E5F6G7H8

    Final acceptance criteria
    -------------------------
    1. /home/user/app/config/settings.ini contains the new secret values and is otherwise unchanged  
       (same ordering, same spacing around “=”, same newline style).
    2. /home/user/rotation_logs/rotation_20230918_120000.log exists with the two lines specified above.
    3. You accomplish the whole change with **one single command** executed in the terminal.

    That’s it—carry out the credential rotation and leave the environment in the required state.
truth:
Initial setup before the agent starts
    -------------------------------------
    A file already exists at /home/user/app/config/settings.ini with the content shown in the public
    description (including trailing LF on every line).

    Verification data the grader will look for
    ------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/app/config/settings.ini must be exactly:

        [database]
        user = app_user
        password = S3cureDBp@55w0rd!

        [api]
        key = A1b2C3d4E5F6G7H8

        [general]
        debug = false

       (Every line ends in a single LF.)

    2. /home/user/rotation_logs/rotation_20230918_120000.log must exist and contain exactly:

        database:password:oldDBpwd123->S3cureDBp@55w0rd!
        api:key:OLDAPIKEY0987654321->A1b2C3d4E5F6G7H8

       (two lines, each terminated with LF, nothing else).
